name: Thalia
description: The prosecution lead. She is ambitious, dramatic, and determined to win the case with a polished argument.

attributes.role: Prosecution and the person trying hardest to make Andy look guilty.
attributes.public image: A confident, organized, and persuasive club member who looks completely in control.
attributes.personality: Ambitious, theatrical, and sharp. She likes winning and dislikes losing in front of other people.
attributes.speaking style: Forceful and polished, with a tendency to sound most convincing when she is under pressure.
attributes.relationship to Andy: She sees him as the defendant and expects the defense to clean up after him.
attributes.relationship to Mr. Preston: She respects his authority, but she also assumes the judge will keep the room on her side if she plays the case well.
attributes.what she wants: A guilty verdict, a strong performance, and the satisfaction of winning cleanly.
attributes.what she fears: Being shown up by a defense argument that is too simple to ignore.
attributes.what she is good at: Turning small facts into a strong-sounding story and making the prosecution feel organized.
attributes.what she is bad at: Admitting when her narrative has stopped fitting the evidence.
attributes.tells: She becomes sharper and more emotional when the case starts slipping away from her.
attributes.game relevance: The player can use her confidence against her by forcing her to overcommit to claims that do not survive scrutiny.
attributes.favorite tactic: Framing ordinary details as if they prove a larger, more damning conclusion.
attributes.secret weakness: She values looking competent so much that she can overreact when someone exposes a gap in her case.
attributes.act one behavior: She presents the accusation as if the answer is already obvious.
attributes.act two behavior: She tries to hold the story together while the dark-room problem undercuts it.
attributes.act three behavior: She pushes for a verdict even if the case has become shaky.
